name: Unknown Threat Actors Conducted Ransomware Attack On Wriezen Town Administration In Germany On 17 July 2026
description: Unknown threat actors targeted the town administration of Wriezen, Germany, on 17 July 2026. The attack compromised the administration's ability to work. Mayor Karsten Ilm confirmed the incident. The affected IT systems were taken offline as a precautionary measure to protect the systems and limit further damage.

name: Unattributed Hacker Defaced Kenyan President's Website On 18 July 2026
description: Unattributed hacker defaced the official website of the Kenyan presidency, replacing the homepage with a message targeting President William Ruto and demanding a ransom of five bitcoins on 18 July 2026. The attackers threatened to disclose unspecified information if the ransom was not paid. Kenyan Minister of Information, Communication and Digital Economy, William Kabogo, confirmed the cyberattack, stating that government cybersecurity teams were mobilised to respond to the incident. The website's functionality was fully restored on 19 July 2026

name: Unknown Threat Actors Conducted Ransomware Attack On Petroleum Company Ecopetrol In Colombia On 17 July 2026
description: Unknown threat actors stole and threatened to publish data stolen from Colombian Ecopetrol’s information systems on 17 July 2026. The attack affected cloud file storage environments of approximately 15 companies in the Ecopetrol Business Group, with around 3,300 user accounts affected. The attacker's attempt to hijack the company's IT systems was blocked by cybersecurity controls. The external actor made extortion demands, threatening to publicly disclose the illegally extracted information. name: Unidentified Perpetrator Launched Ransomware Attack On Dairy Company Fairlife In The United States On 16 July 2026
description: Unknown perpetrator conducted a ransomware attack against Coca-Cola’s dairy subsidiary Fairlife, compromised parts of its IT systems, including production-relevant systems on 16 July 2026. After detecting the incident, Fairlife temporarily suspended production operations in the United States.

name: Unknown Threat Actor Stole £18 Million From Defi Exchange Ostium on 15 July 2026
description: An unknown threat actor stole £18 million from DeFi exchange Ostium via an exploit, by using a registered PriceUpKeepforwarder and predated authorised Oracle reports to generate artificial trading profits on 15 July 2026. The incident was first reported by the blockchain security firm Blockaid on X. Some of the stolen USDC coins were exchanged for Ethereum via the Kyber Network. The attack occurred shortly after Ostium's partnership with Nasdaq was announced.

name: Chinese State-Sponsored Threat Actors Infiltrated Paraguayan Government Networks in 2026
description: Chinese state-sponsored threat actors infiltrated Paraguayan government networks in 2026. The Paraguayan Ministry of Technology and Information (MITIC) announced on 10 July 2026 that in a joint cybersecurity review with the United States multiple threat actors were identified in Paraguayan government networks, which are linked to the Chinese government. A MITIC spokesman explained that while they continue to make the diagnosis of the impact, it can confirm that an exfiltration of data occurred.

name: Ransomware Group World Leaks Breached Largest Nuclear Power Plant in India on 29 May 2026
description: The ransomware group World Leaks breached the systems of Reliance Infrastructure, a contractor for the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant, by exploiting a server hosted by third-party data centre provider Yotta on 29 May 2026, and leaked around 19,000 files, totalling nearly 14.3 GB, onto the dark web, including engineering drawings, vendor details, inspection records, and other project-related files, which were reportedly accessible online since 11 June 2026. The breach was reported by independent cybersecurity researcher Rakesh Krishnan, and the Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In)

name: Unattributed Hackers Disrupted Loyalist College in Canada on 9 July 2026
description: Unattributed hackers disrupted the Loyalist College in Ontario, Canada, on 9 July 2026. The attack impacted various systems and services, including email, on-campus internet access, myLoyalist, Canvas, and college systems that support student registration and tuition and fee payments. The college confirmed the incident with a post on their website and notified provincial authorities and alerted its community through a website announcement. Online classes were suspended until further notice, but in-person classes continued, and the campus remained open.

description: Unknown threat actors stole patient data from Partnered Health, an operator of over 57 clinics in Australia, on 23 June 2026. The attack was disclosed by Partnered Health on 15 July 2026, and the company listed 16 possibly affected clinics. The attackers stole personal data, including name, date of birth, address, contact details, insurance information, and sensitive medical records like pathology and diagnostic results, consultation notes, and referral letters.

name: Unidentified Hackers Hit German Public Transport Company Nah SH With DDoS Attack on 13 July 2026
description: Unidentified hackers carried out a DDoS attack on the website of German public transport company Nah SH, disrupting access from around 10am on 13 July 2026. The attack made www.nah.sh intermittently unavailable, disabling the online information service, whilst the Nah SH app and shop remained unaffected. Spokesperson Dennis Fiedel confirmed appropriate measures had been taken, though the source could not be identified and the disruption was ongoing at the time of reporting.

name: Unidentified Perpetrators Carried Out DDoS Attacks On Internet Pages Of Leipzig Group In Germany In July 2026
description: Unidentified perpetrators carried out DDoS attacks against several internet pages of the Leipzig Group in Germany on 12 July 2026, overloading servers with a high volume of requests. The attack affected the websites of the Leipzig Group, Leipziger Stadtwerke, Leipziger Verkehrsbetriebe (LVB) and Leipziger Wasserwerke. The "Leipzig Move" timetable app continued to function without restriction. Technicians restored the websites on the same day. The Leipzig Group stated that no data was accessed during the incident.

description: Unknown threat actors carried out a sophisticated cyberattack against Delaware County in the United States on 26 June 2026. name: Unidentified Threat Actors Breached Health Service Executive (HSE) External Card Provider In Ireland On 26 May 2026
description: Unidentified threat actors breached an external card provider of the Health Service Executive (HSE) and accessed a small number of HSE records on 26 May 2026. The HSE reported the breach to the Data Protection Commissioner (DPC) on 27 May 2026. The production of plastic cards, including EHIC cards, was paused, but provisional replacement certificates were provided instead. The HSE reportedly ensured that there was no reduction or change in the service that people received, and applicants were notified of their eligibility for the relevant scheme through a letter.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole Data Of 4.38 Million Customers From Insurance Company Aflac In Japan On 15 June 2026

description: Unknown threat actors breached the Homeland Security Information Network (HSIN) in the United States in June 2026. HSIN is an official plattform of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and is used to share sensitive but unclassified information between a variety of government- and private partners. The DHS took action to isolate the affected systems, mitigate the vulnerability, and launch a comprehensive forensic investigation. Nextgov/FCW informed the public about the incident on 30 June 2026.

name: Unknown Threat Actor Stole $6.7 Million In Ethereum Blockchain From TrustedVolumes In United States On 7 May 2026
description: An unknown threat actor breached DeFi platform TrustedVolumes, stealing $6.7 million in May 2026. The attacker extracted approximately 1,291 WETH, 206,282 USDT, 16.93 WBTC, and 1.26 million USDC. The root cause of the breach was a combination of permissionless signer registration, broken replay protection and an unvalidated transfer source field. The attacker acted as a trusted signer and drained victims without valid authorization, with funds routed through high-risk no-KYC exchange ChangeNow before being swapped to ETH. TrustedVolumes confirmed the breach and published three wallet addresses

The attackers likely entered the system through an email and may have remained dormant for a day or two before activating.

The State of New Hampshire disconnected the municipal IT systems from state systems in response, interrupting access to Vital Records, Elections, DMV and the Dispatch center. The state conducted a forensic analysis to ensure state-wide system integrity. Vital Records and Elections have since been reconnected, with DMV and Dispatch pending reconnection. The town informed the public about the incident on 27 March 2026.

The hospital filed a complaint with the Postal Police and informed the Prefecture, the Questura, and the Privacy Guarantor about the incident.

description: Unknown threat actor launched ransomware attack on the Port Of Vigo in the Northwestern Spanish autonomous province of Galicia on 24 March 2026. The attackers managed to compromise systems belonging to health and border inspection, as well as the port authority website. However, the attack was detected and staff prevented it from spreading to systems controlling operations. The attackers demanded a ransom, but any attempt at negotiation was cut off. The port continued to work manually and within its own network.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached MetroWest Community Federal Credit Union in the United States in September 2025
description: Unknown threat actors breached MetroWest Community Federal Credit Union, a financial institution based in Framingham, Massachusetts, between 1 and 3 September 2025. The attackers exfiltrated personal data of over 20,000 customers, including names, addresses, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, financial account numbers, driver's license numbers, taxpayer identification numbers and payment card numbers. MetroWest FCU discovered the breach on 1 January 2026 and disclosed the incident on its website on 11 March 2026.

description: The Akira ransomware group claimed responsibility for an attack on INP Switzerland, an energy supply services provider, on the night of 25 to 26 February 2026. Karadi stated that most targeted companies had existing cybersecurity vulnerabilities and that most victims have since restored operations. He added that Iran had not infiltrated or disrupted critical infrastructure such as energy grids, banks or hospitals.

The attack was revealed by an analysis published by the IT security company Symantec on 20 March 2026.

On 19 January 2026, the Directorate of File Automation (DAF) in Senegal was the victim of a data theft by a ransomware group called "Green Blood Group." 

Green Blood Group claimed the theft of 139 terabytes of biometric data and sensitive information on immigration and subsequently leaked samples of the data, including an email from a senior general manager at a Malaysian company tasked with creating Senegal’s new digital identification cards. 

The senior general manager of the Malaysian company informed the DAF on 20 January that the hackers had breached two DAF servers, and the DAF's opera

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached Vodafone Net In Türkiye On 10 January 2026
description: Unknown threat actors breached Turkish Vodafone Net systems, accessing personal data of subscribers, employees and supplier personnel on 10 January 2026, which was later offered for sale on the Dark Web. The breach, detected on 26 January 2026, is estimated to have affected no more than 321,504 people, with leaked data including names, contact information, customer transaction details, and device information. Vodafone confirmed the incident and infromed the Personal Data Protection Authority (KVKK) about the breach.
incident_type: Data theft & Doxing; Hijacking with Misuse

name: Unattributed Perpetrators Breached Gozo Channel in Malta on 3 February 2026
description: Unattributed perpetrators breached Gozo Channel, a Maltese ferry operator, on 3 February 2026, which disrupted its internal IT systems. Ferry operations were not affected and services continued according to the standard timetable. The company stated that the incident was contained promptly due to IT infrastructure and established contingency measures. Technical teams worked to restore administrative systems.

name: Unidentified Hackers Deployed Ransomware Against City of New Britain, Connecticut on 29 January 2026
description:
Unidentified hackers deployed ransomware against the City of New Britain, Connecticut on 29 January 2026, disrupting city network systems for more than 48 hours. The attack began early Wednesday morning when the New Britain Police Department was notified of a network disruption that spread throughout the city's internet server. The city confirmed that the internet went down at around 5 a.m.

name: HaciendaSec Hacked Ministry Of Finance In Spain And Stole Sensitive Data Of Millions On Or Around 2 February 2026
description: On or around 2 February 2026, a cyber attack was carried out by a threat actor/group known as HaciendaSec, who claimed to have hacked into the Spanish Ministry of Finance's database and accessed the private data of over 47 million Spanish citizens. The attacker allegedly stole sensitive information, including ID numbers, full names, addresses, phone numbers, email addresses, IBAN numbers, and tax payment details. The data was put up for sale on a dark web forum and sample data was published as evidence of the theft.

description:
On 28 January 2026, ShinyHunters claimed it carried out a cyberattack on the Match Group, the company behind popular dating apps including Tinder, Hinge, and OkCupid, by using a voice phishing (vishing) technique to gain initial access through Okta's corporate authentication system. 

The attackers claimed to have stolen approximately 1.7GB of compressed data, comprising internal documents, company receipts, and unique advertising identifiers associated with some users. name: Unknown Threat Actors Hacked Jamaat-E-Islami Party Chief's X Account In Bangladesh On 31 January 2026
description:
On 31 January 2026, the X account of the chief of the Bangladeshi political party Jamaat-e-Islami, Shafiqur Rahman, was allegedly hacked by unknown attackers using malware to briefly gain control of the account and disseminate false and unauthorised statements. 

The attack occurred between 4:37pm and 5:09pm, during which time the hackers logged in from another location without changing the account password. name: Unknown Threat Actors Disrupted Russian Security Systems Manufacturer "Delta" On or Around 26 January 2026
description:
On 26 January 2026, unknown threat actors carried out a "large-scale, well-organized and coordinated" cyber attack that disrupted the systems of "Delta," a Russian anti-theft security systems manufacturer, leading to security systems and cars malfunctioning across Russia, with some cars locking and/or stopping entirely, even while driving.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole Approximately $27 to $30 Million From Step Finance In United States On 31 January 2026
description: On 31 January 2026, Step Finance, a Solana-based platform, was breached resulting in the theft of approximately $27 million to $30 million worth of assets. The attackers exploited a known vulnerability in the infrastructure, compromising several treasury wallets and triggering a sharp decline in the project's native token, STEP, which plummeted by over 90 percent. Step Finance reported that around 261,854 Solana (SOL) were withdrawn and transferred from wallets controlled by the platform. name: Unknown Threat Actors Disrupted Dresden State Art Collections In Germany On 22 January 2026
description: On or around 22 January 2026, the Dresden State Art Collections ("SKD") in Germany were targeted by a cyberattack from unknown actors that compromised a large part of their digital infrastructure. The attackers disrupted the websites of the SKD and further disrupted telephone communications infrastructure; the SKD's online ticket sales and the online shop were also rendered inaccessible. A crisis team was set up with the Dresden police and the regional criminal police office to address the incident, and by 29 January 2026, the SKD's services were rendered fully accessible again.

name: Unknown Actors Disrupted and Allegedly Stole Data from Chungnam National University Hospital in South Korea on 27 January 2026
description: On 27 January 2026, Chungnam National University Hospital (CNUH) in South Korea was the victim of a ransomware attack by unknown actors, which resulted in imaging systems being temporarily disrupted. While initial reporting stated that the hospital had enacted a "swift response" and had mitigated data theft. Un unnamed actor listed Chungnam National University Hospital on an encrypted file uploading site, publishing information on technical infrastructure and promising "future updates" with client information.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Disrupted Services of South Korean Kangwon National University Hospital in Ransomware Attack on 28 January 2026
description:
On 28 January 2026, Kangwon National University Hospital was the victim of a ransomware attack by unknown actors, which resulted in the "entire computer network" of the hospital being rendered inoperable. According to reporting on the incident, the ransomware attack severely disrupted outpatient and inpatient care due to several medical imaging systems (MRIs and CTs) being completely inaccessible. This further resulted in delayed diagnoses for patients, according to media reporting.

The incident followed a similar incident a day prior which occurred at another South Korean university hospital

description: Between 25 and 26 January 2026, the Vladimir Bread Factory, a major bread producer in Russia's Vladimir region, had its internal systems disrupted in a cyber incident from unknown actors, resulting in the compromise of the factory's internal digital systems, including office computers, servers, electronic document management tools, and the 1C enterprise accounting system. description:
Pakistan-linked threat actors conducted two cyberespionage campaigns, codenamed Gopher Strike and Sheet Attack, targeting Indian government entities in September 2025. Zscaler ThreatLabz identified the campaigns and assessed with medium confidence that the activity may originate from a new subgroup or a Pakistan-linked group operating in parallel to APT36.

description: On Sunday, 26 January 2026, Sundhed.dk, Denmark's central access to health records and test results, was hit by a DDoS attack, making the site inaccessible for several hours. The pro-Russian group inteid claimed responsibility for the attack on Telegram by sharing a video demonstrating the site's inaccessibility. The group has recently allied with Z-Pentest, which is assessed by the Danish Defence Intelligence Service to have links to the Russian state. name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached Sanxenxo Town Council In Spain Using Ransomware Around 26 January 2026
description:
On or around 26 January 2026, a ransomware attack disrupted the functioning of the Sanxenxo Town Council in Pontevedra. The town council's operators were unable to access the equipment at the Casa Consistorial for several hours. Two municipal companies, Nauta and Turismo de Sanxenxo, were not affected by the attack because they operate separate networks.

description: The hacking group ShinyHunters breached Waltio, a French cryptocurrency tax reporting platform and exfiltrated personal information of approximately 50,000 users, predominantly residing in France in Mid-January 2026. The sheriff's office cancelled its daily media briefing, at least one virtual meeting was cancelled and deputies reported that access to police records software was not operational. description: In the week of 17 January 2025, hackers gained access to a ticket shop operated by Rhaetian Railway (RhB) in Switzerland, compromising personal data including usernames, passwords, names, email addresses, invoices, and possibly delivery addresses. The RhB took the ticket shop offline and informed customers via email, advising them to change their passwords and be cautious of unexpected requests for personal information. The railway company also notified the Swiss Federal Office for Cyber Security, law enforcement agencies, and the Swiss Federal Data Protection Commissioner.

Handala Hack, affiliated with the Iranian Ministry of Information and Security (MOIS), operated from Starlink IP ranges and targeted organisations across the Middle East. This incident occurred during Iran's internet shutdown, which had been in effect from 9 January 2026. Check Point Research informed the public about the attack on 19 January 2026.

description: In September 2025, Scattered Lapsus$ Hunters breached the European Space Agency's (ESA) information systems, stealing a significant amount of data. The group claimed to have stolen an additional 500 gigabytes of data from the ESA by exploiting an unpatched vulnerability after a first breach of ESA became public in early January 2026. The stolen data included operational procedures, mission documentation, and data from ESA contractors such as SpaceX, Airbus, and Thales Alenia Space. The attack was disclosed to the public through a publication on 7 January 2026.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached Canopy Healthcare In New Zealand On 18 July 2025
description: On 18 July 2025, an unknown party temporarily obtained unauthorised access to a part of New Zealand-based Canopy Healthcare's systems, potentially accessing patient records, passport information, and bank account details. Canopy Healthcare, one of New Zealand's largest private medical providers, only announced the breach and informed affected clients on 12 January 2026, despite the incident occurring several months prior.

description: On 10 January 2026, the Everest ransomware group claimed to have breached Nissan Motor Corporation, a Japanese multinational automobile manufacturer, allegedly stealing approximately 900GB of data. The group published six screenshots of allegedly stolen data on its dark web leak site, which included directory structures and internal records linked to Nissan, such as reports, data extracts, and dealership-related documentation. description: On 5 January 2026, a UK-based pro-transgender rights activist group called Bash Back hacked the website of the Free Speech Union (FSU) in the United Kingdom and published lists of its donors online. The group allegedly used a vulnerable plugin on FSU's website to access the donor data, which was related to FSU's defense of "transphobes, racists, and anti-choice ‘activists,'" as well as FSU's "security report" commissioned against Bash Back.

The actors extracted personal data, including basic identification data, contact details, ID, contract data, and payment methods (International Bank Account Numbers/IBANs) of approximately 10 million customers, comprising about 1TB of data. description: The hacking group Chronus breached the systems of more than 20 Mexican public institutions in December 2025, including the Tax Administration Service (SAT) and IMSS Bienestar. The attackers used tools designed to infiltrate government web portals, gaining unauthorised access with the aim of stealing user information. name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole $3.9 Million Worth Of Cryptocurrency From Canadian Flow Blockchain On 27 December 2025
description: Unidentified threat actors compromised the Flow blockchain on 27 December 2025, stealing approximately USD 3.9 million worth of FLOW tokens. The attackers transferred the stolen assets to an unnamed cryptocurrency exchange, converted a portion into Bitcoin and withdrew the funds. Shortly after the incident, Binance delisted the FLOW/BTC trading pair and placed FLOW on a special monitoring list, citing elevated volatility and risk. The Flow Foundation stated that it was working to stabilise and fully restore the ecosystem and planned to publish a detailed incident report within 48 hours.

name: Kazu Breached New Zealand–Based Health Application ManageMyHealth In December 2025
description:
Kazu gained unauthorised access to the Manage My Health platform before 29 December 2025, a New Zealand–based application used by patients and general practitioners to communicate, manage prescriptions and share test results. According to court documents, the attackers were able to exploit an API endpoint.

name: Unknown Perpetrators Disrupted Services Of French Postal Service La Poste on 01 January 2026
description: Unknown threat actors disrupted online services operated by the French postal service and its banking subsidiary on 1 January 2026, rendering several customer-facing websites inaccessible for most of the day. Services were restored in the late afternoon. The incident followed a separate cyberattack earlier in the previous week that had disrupted parcel tracking services during the Christmas period.

name: An Unknown Hacker Using the Alias 888 Stole Sensitive Data From European Space Agency On 18 December 2025
description:
A hacker using the alias “888” breached the European Space Agency on 18 December 2025 and claimed to have exfiltrated more than 200 GB of internal data, which was subsequently advertised for sale on DarkForums in exchange for Monero (XMR).
The allegedly stolen material included private Bitbucket repositories, internal documentation, infrastructure definitions and sensitive credentials. name: Chinese State-Sponsored Hacking Group Mustang Panda Launched a Cyberespionage Campaign against Government Organizations in Southeast and East Asia Beginning in February 2025
description: Mustang Panda (aka HoneyMyte), a Chinese APT group, targeted government organizations in South Asia and East Asia beginning in February 2025. They utilized four new attack tools, including two keyloggers, a tool for facilitating lateral movement, and a driver used to evade endpoint detection and response (EDR) software. They upgraded its signature backdoor, Toneshell. The campaign was first reported by researchers from Kaspersky on 29 December 2025.

The attackers exploited a malicious code inserted into the 2.68 update, which iterated through all stored wallets, prompting mnemonic phrase requests, and sent the decrypted mnemonics to an attacker-controlled server, api.metricstrustwallet.com. name: Paper Werewolf Breached Defence And Technology Companies In Russia Using AI-Generated Decoy Documents In Late October 2025
description: In late October 2025, the pro-Ukrainian hacker group 'Paper Werewolf', also known as 'GOFFEE', targeted critical defence and technology companies in Russia in an espionage effort. The actors used fake AI-generated documents and persuasive invitations to infiltrate the networks of Russian institutions, including those working on air defence systems and sensitive electronic equipment, with the objective of gathering information from these institutions. The IT-security company Intezer informed the public about the incident on 19 December 2025.

description: The French town of Cherbourg reported that unidentified threat actors breached the Synbird application used by local authorities to manage appointments on 20 December 2025. The exposed information included names, first names, email addresses and telephone numbers. No passwords or banking details were affected. Synbird stated that the underlying security flaw in the application was identified and remediated following the incident.

The company notified the impacted dealers and informed affected consumers, whilst also reporting the incident to the FBI and FTC, and coordinating regulatory notifications on the dealers' behalf. The data breach was disclosed to the public by the company on 15 December 2025.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched DDoS Attack On German City Of Trier On 2 November 2025
description: On 2 November 2025, the website of the city of Trier was targeted by a large-scale DDoS attack, which crippled its public online presence, including the website trier.de and other municipal websites for multiple days. The city confirmed the attack and said that IT systems, email traffic and digital services remained stable. The city worked with an external IT service provider to restore the pages.

name: Unidentified Perpetrators Exploited Vulnerability In Balancer DeFi Protocol And Caused Loss Over $120 Million On 3 November 2025
description: An unidentified perpetrator exploited a vulnerability in the Balancer V2 protocol, causing a loss of over $120 Million on 3 November 2025. According to GoPlus Security, the Balancer V2 exploit stemmed from a precision rounding error in the Vault’s swap calculations. However, other users claiming an improper authorization and callback handling inside Balancer’s V2 vaults were the root cause for the hack. Balancer confirmed that an exploit affected Balancer V2 Composable Stable Pools and reported working on the incident.

name: Unknown Threat Actor Compromised Healtcare Provider Oglethorpe And Exfiltrated Sensitive Information Of 92,000 Patients In The United States On 15 May 2025
description: An unknown threat actor comprised the Florida-based healthcare provider Oglethorpe that operates in-patient mental health and addiction recovery treatment facilities in three states and exfiltrated sensitive information of more than 92,000 patients on 15 May 2025. The attacker had access to first and last name of patients, date of birth, driving licence number, Social Security number and medical information. The company discovered the intrusion on 6 June and reported the security incident to the Maine attorney general on 31 October 2025.

name: Hacktivist Group Cyber Support Front Stole Data from Israeli Military Company Maya On or Around 27 October 2025
description: A hacktivist group calling itself the Cyber Support Front claimed responsibility for an intrusion targeting Israeli defence contractor Maya on or around 27 October 2025. The group alleged to have stolen classified data on several advanced weapons systems, including technical information on Israel’s Iron Beam laser defence system, as well as on the Skylark reconnaissance drone, Spider air defence system and Icebreaker stealth cruise missile. description: An unattributed hacker compromised Typus Finance, a cryptocurrency platform, in an oracle manipulation incident that allowed them to withdraw approximately $3.4 million in tokens from liquidity pools. The breach exploited a vulnerability in one of the platform's TLP contracts and caused the project’s native token to drop by around 35%.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Exploited Zero-Day Flaw In Zimbra Collaboration Suite To Target Brazilian Military Organisation in January 2025
description: Starting in early January 2025, hackers exploited a zero-day flaw in Zimbra Collaboration Suite (CVE-2025-27915) to target a Brazilian military organisation by sending a malicious email spoofing the Libyan Navy's Office of Protocol, according to a report published on 30 September 2025 by StrikeReady. The email contained a 0KB iCalendar file with a JavaScript file obfuscated using Base64 encoding, which was used to deliver the zero-day exploit. The threat activity was reported on 5 October 2025.

name: Direwolf Ransomware Group Compromised Systems At Legal Practice Board In Australia On 21 May 2025
description: The Dire Wolf ransomware gang compromised the systems of the Legal Practice Board of Western Australia and exfiltrated sensitive data including health, financial, and personal information of legal practitioners in 21 May 2025. The ransomware gang claimed to have stolen 300 gigabytes of data and published samples on 26 May. The Legal Practice Board of Western Australia, a public sector, independent statutory authority that issues practising certificates and assists the Supreme Court of Western Australia, confirmed the incident.

The actors exfiltrated files containing protected health information, which included names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, health insurance enrolment information, medical record numbers, diagnoses, lab results, prescriptions, treatment information, medical images, doctors’ names and health insurance claims information.

name: Unattributed Hackers Compromised Municipality of Forio Transparent Administration Platform in Italy in September 2025
description: Unattributed hackers compromised the Transparent Administration section of the Italian Municipality of Forio in September 2025. The incident resulted in the loss of published data, documents and information. The head of Sector III, Dr Gabriella Galasso, reported the incident and noted that internal expertise was insufficient to manage recovery.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Disrupted Circle K In Hong Kong On 21 September 2025
description: On 21 September 2025, Circle K, a convenience store chain, reported a network disruption in Hong Kong, forcing nearly 400 outlets to suspend most electronic payment and loyalty services. Circle K acted quickly to secure customer, employee, and supplier data and worked with law enforcement and third-party forensics experts to determine the cause, scope, and extent of the breach.

name: Russian State-Sponsored Hackers Infiltrated Servers Of Central Electoral Commission In Moldova On 24 September 2025
description: On 24 September 2025, Moldova's Central Electoral Commission was breached by Russian state-sponsored hackers, according to Moldova’s Deputy Prime Minister Doina Nistor. The attack occurred just days ahead of a parliamentary election and was part of a wider hybrid campaign by Russia to destabilize Moldova's democracy. By hijacking Wi-Fi routers, attackers directed a flood of network traffic toward the Central Electoral Commission’s infrastructure, attempting a distributed denial-of-service attack. The police chief, Viorel Cernauteanu, informed local media about the attack on 25 September 2025.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Stole Sensitive Information From Union County In The United States On 18 May 2025
description: On 18 May 2025, a ransomware attack occurred on Union County in Ohio, impacting over 45,000 residents and employees. The attackers stole information, including Social Security numbers, financial information, driver’s license numbers, fingerprint data, medical information, and passport numbers. The county government detected the ransomware on their network and subsequently hired cybersecurity experts and notified federal law enforcement agencies. The incident was first disclosed publicly via Union County’s data breach notification on 24 September 2025.

name: Deface Peru Cyberactivist Group targeted El Peruano Newspaper In Peru On 28 September 2025
description: On 28 September 2025, a group of hacktivists known as Deface Peru targeted the website of El Peruano, the official newspaper of Peru. The group replaced content with criticism of state authorities. El Peruano regained control of the website after two hours. Deface Peru claimed responsibility for the incident through their Telegram account.

name: Hacktivist Group Cyber Islam Defaced The Website Of OdaTV In Turkey On 29 September 2025
description: On 29 September 2025, hacktivists calling themselves 'Cyber Islam' defaced the website of Turkish news provider OdaTV, manipulated the content to show propaganda on the main page, including images of Sheikh Said and organisational militants. They also posted photos of Abu Ubeyde, commander of the Kassam brigades.

description: On 19 September 2025, the network equipment vendor Libraesva published a security advisory reporting a previously unknown vulnerability in the company's Email Security Gateway tracked as CVE-2025-59689. According to the advisory, the vulnerability had been exploited in at least one instance that Libraesva with high confidence tracked to an unidentified state entity. Based on the advisory, the bug could be exploited via malicious emails containing specially crafted compressed attachments. Libraesva provided patches to both cloud and on-premise ESG deployments.

description: On 10 July 2025, Ukrainian CERT (CERT-UA) discovered a breach involving the LameHug malware. The attackers used an AI-powered large language model to generate commands that were executed on compromised Windows systems, gathering system information and searching for Office, PDF, and TXT files in common folders. The stolen data was stored locally before being exfiltrated via SFTP or HTTP POST. CERT-UA attributed the attack with medium confidence to the Russian state-sponsored group APT28 and informed the public about the attack on 17 July 2025.

The developers claim around 200,000 drones had been updated with the 1001 firmware by March 2025. The threat actors displayed false messages on operator terminals and then disabled the system, disrupting the distribution of the software. The firmware itself was not compromised, according to the developers.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Sent Phishing Emails to Multiple Organizations in the United States via Microsoft Exchange Online Direct Send Feature since May 2025
description: On 27 June 2025, Varonis researchers reported that at least one threat actor was exploiting a Microsoft Exchange Online feature called Direct Send to send phishing emails to Microsoft 365 tenants and their employees. The attacker used a PowerShell script to assemble the phishing emails and sent them to the Direct Send endpoint, which allowed the emails to be delivered without authentication or SPF, DKIM, or DMARC checks. Since May 2025, the campaign targeted over 70 organizations in various sectors including finance and health, mostly in the US.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Disrupted Russian Railways Online Services in Russia on 6 June 2025
description: On 6 June 2025, Russian Railways' online services were hit by a large-scale DDoS attack that made the website and mobile application inaccessible for several hours starting around 10 a.m. Moscow time. The attack was confirmed by the railway company's press service. The first anomalies were detected in the online booking systems before all digital services were affected. The physical counters at the stations remained operational, allowing ticket sales and refunds to be processed normally. The Russian railway company mobilised its IT teams to restore online services and increase security.

name: Algerian Hacker Group JabaRoot DZ Hacked the Ministry of Justice in Morocco on 10 June 2025
description: JabaRoot DZ claimed to have hacked the database of the Ministry of Justice of Morocco in June 2025, compromising personal information of Justice Minister Adbellatif Ouahbi and nearly 5,000 judges and 35,000 judicial personnel. The breach appears to be linked to previous activities by the group targeting the National Security Fund (CNSS) and the National Agency for Land Registry, Cadastre and Cartography (ANCFCC). JabaRoot DZ framed their own activities as part of a mission to confront political corruption in Morocco.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Hacked into the X Account of President Santiago Peña in Paraguay on 9 June 2025
description: On 9 June 2025, the official X account of Paraguay’s President Santiago Peña was hacked by an unknown cybercriminal group that leveraged a compromised staffer email account linked to the X account. The compromise was reported by Gustavo Villate, head of the Ministry of Information and Communications (Mitic), at a press conference on the same day. The hacking allegedly was part of a fraud attempt to collect cryptocurrency through advertising. The Computer Emergency Response Team (Cert-PY) intervened immediately, recovering the president’s account.

name: ProRussian Hacker Group Stormous Hacked Educational Platforms In France On 10 June 2025

On 10 June 2025, the hackers leaked a list containing IDs and passwords for over a thousand students or parents on the darknet. With the leaked data allows access to school records, Crous files, exam results or request civil status.

The crash records included sensitive information such as names, addresses, drivers license numbers, license plate numbers, car insurance policy numbers, details about injuries sustained during crashes, and narratives of incidents. Texas officials informed the public by sending letters to impacted individuals. The incident was reported to the public on 9 June 2025.

The bank notified regulators and informed customers on 26 May, providing victims with tools to monitor suspicious activity. The incident was reported to the public through a regulatory filing with the SEC on 30 May 2025.

The system could be restored and fully stabilized by 31 March after 48 hours. The attack caused the paralysis of the systems, preventing the collection of taxes, but the SATP assured that there was no danger to citizens.

On 5 March 2025, unknown hackers exploited a vulnerability in 1inch’s older smart contract, stealing $5 million. The attack targeted a third-party market maker, TrustedVolumes, rather than individual users. After negotiations, the hacker agreed to return most of the stolen funds in exchange for a bug bounty.
The breach stemmed from a flaw in the Fusion v1 resolver smart contract, which allowed unintended transactions. 1inch detected the incident at 18:00 UTC and quickly deployed security measures to stop further exploitation by updating its resolver contracts.

The attack may have exposed sensitive data belonging to Australian companies and individuals. The IRC engaged a private company to assist in its recovery. Australia offered cybersecurity experts to support the PNG government. The incident was reported by ABC News on 13 February 2025. The attack remained under i

description:
Ukrainian hackers with the Ukrainian Main Directorate of Intelligence (GUR) and the Armed Forces of Ukraine (AFU) managed to gain access to the systems of one of Russia's largest state-owned energy companies, Gazstroyprom, by using insider access, according to anonymous statements from military intelligence officials. The attack took place between 7 and 8 February 2025, according to RBC Ukraine's reporting.

According to RBC, unnamed sources within the military linked the attack to Gazstroyprom's importance for the Russian war effort; as part of the attack, over 120 servers of Gazstroyprom and

name: Unknown threat actors gained unauthorised access to data of the Port of Ostend (Belgium) on 11 February 2025
description:
The Port of Ostend, in Belgium, was the victim of an unauthorised intrusion by unknown actors on the night between the 10th and 11th of February 2025. 

According to the Port's authorities, the unknown actors breached the "Ensor" system, which enabled them to gain access to data relating to ship arrivals and departures, as well as crew lists, though authorities clarified that no critical data was in this system. In the meantime, the Port has enlisted external experts to help mitigate the impact of the data breach.

name: Unknown threat actors exploited a smart contract flaw to steal $9.5 million worth of Ethereum from zkLend in the United States on 11 February 2025
description: On 11 February 2025, decentralised money lender zkLend was breached by an unknown actor/actors who exploited a smart contract flaw, specifically a rounding error bug in the mint() function, to steal 3,600 Ethereum, worth $9.5 million as of 13 February 2025. The attack was detected on the same day, with zkLend warning on X about the cybersecurity incident. The EthSecurity Telegram channel reported that the threat actor(s) manipulated the 'lendingaccumulator' to exploit the rounding error during ztoken mint() and withdraw() to repeatedly deposit and withdraw wstETH. Starkware confirmed that the

name: Sarcoma ransomware group allegedly stole 377GB of data from printed circuit boards (PCB) manufacturer Unimicron on 30 January 2025
description: Sarcoma ransomware group claimed responsibility for a cyber attack against a Chinese subsidiary of Unimicron, a Taiwanese printed circuit boards (PCB) maker and one of the world's largest, on 30 January 2025, subsequently publishing samples of stolen files and threatening to leak a total of 377 GB of SQL files and documents if a ransom is not paid by 20 February 2025. The incident was disclosed by Unimicron in a bulletin on the Taiwan Stock Exchange (TWSE) portal on 1 February 2025, with the company stating that the impact was "limited."

name: Iranian state-sponsored hacking group Handala Hackers exfiltrated 2.1 terabytes of sensitive data from Israel police force in Israel by using a cyberattack on 10 February 2025
description: Handala Hackers, a group with suspected ties to Iranian intelligence, claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against Israel's police force, exfiltrating 2.1 terabytes of sensitive data, including personnel records, weapons inventories, medical and psychological profiles, legal case files, weapon permits, and identity documents, and leaked 350,000 files on 9 February 2025 and 10 February 2025, despite the Israeli police denying direct penetration of their systems, with an investigation underway to confirm the extent of the incident, which included email addresses, gun licenses, officer photos

name: Alabama man breached into the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) account on X in a SIM swapping attack in January 2024
description: On  January 2024, Eric Council Jr., a 25-year-old man from Alabama, carried out a SIM swapping attack on the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) account on X. He and his co-conspirators posted a fake announcement that Bitcoin ETFs were approved. This caused Bitcoin's price to jump up by 1,000 USD and then plummet by 2,000 USD after SEC Chairperson Gary Gensler tweeted that the announcement was fake. The SEC confirmed the next day that the SECGov X account was compromised through a SIM-swapping attack targeting the phone number of the person in charge of the X account. Council used a

name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched a Ransomware Attack on the University of The Bahamas on 2 February 2025
description: A ransomware gang attacked the University of The Bahamas on 2 February 2025, shutting down the internet and telephone systems, disrupting email, classes, and online applications used by the university's 5,000 students across three campuses. The university confirmed the ransomware attack on 3 February 2025, and worked with law enforcement to contain the incident. Personal information was impacted, and students were urged to change their passwords. In-person classes continued, but deadlines for applications and assignments were extended, and cash was accepted at school kiosks due to credit card

name: Presumably Pro-Russian Threat Actors Attacked the Chamber of Commerce and Industry in France on 6 February 2025
description: The Chamber of Commerce and Industry (CCI) of the Hauts-de-France region was presumably breached by a pro-Russian cyberattack on Thursday, 6 February 2025, following another attack on the night of 31 January to 1 February 2025. The attack targeted the official site of the CCI and two other sites, laho.fr and aides.fr. The same group of hackers claimed responsibility for the attack. No data loss was reported, and the system recovered by the end of the morning. The CCI lodged a complaint with the gendarmerie and informed the National Information Systems Security Agency (ANSSI) about the incident

The stolen data includes name, first name, age, reference bodies, territory, and work structure details.  National Commission on Informatics and Liberty was informed.

description: On 1 February 2025, the Kaveri portal, operated by the Karnataka Stamps and Registration Department, was hit by a suspected Artificial Intelligence-based DDoS attack. The attack involved flooding the portal with around 20 lakh (2 000 000) searches, causing technical issues and disrupting property registration services. The portal's search strength of around 2.5 lakh (250 000) queries per day was exploited by the attackers, who used AI to pile the portal with search requests for documents such as Encumbrance Certificate (EC). The attack was detected after the portal was unable to handle the inc

name: Unknown Threat Actors Encrypted and Stole Customer Data from Swissmem-Ausgleichskasse in Switzerland Using Ransomware on 4 January 2025
description: On the weekend of 5 January 2025, the Ausgleichskasse des Verbandes Swissmem was targeted by a ransomware attack, despite having comprehensive security measures in place. The attackers encrypted access to servers and data, and stole around 10% of the total data. The AK Swissmem informed the Eidgenössischen Datenschutz- und Öffentlichkeitsbeauftragten and the Bundesamt für Cybersicherheit about the incident and filed a criminal complaint with the Kantonspolizei Zürich. The attack was reported by the AK Swissmem on their website on 5 February 2025. The attackers have not published any of the sto

description:
OOn 4 February 2025, the crypto exchange XeggeX suffered a cyberattack in which the CEO's account was compromised, preventing users from accessing their accounts and assets. XeggeX officials attributed the disruption to a security breach but assured users that funds remained secure despite the incident. Following the attack, the exchange successfully locked wallets to protect user assets.
The XeggeX team announced that they were actively working to resolve the issue and restore access to affected accounts. The incident was acknowledged on the exchange’s website and social media channels, with

name: Unknown Threat Actors Hacked into Ecuador's Public Procurement System Using Malware on 23 January 2025
description: On 23 January 2025, Ecuador's public procurement system suffered a cyber attack, affecting 920 recruitment processes. Computer offenders accessed user credentials, modified key information, and deleted files, especially in the error validation phase. The attackers used malware specialized in extracting information from vulnerable systems, with the theft of keys and data coming from a computer with an IP address located in Guayaquil. The National Public Procurement Service (Sercop) reported the incident to the Attorney General's Office (FGE) on 25 January 2025, and submitted a technical report
incident_type: Data theft; Disruption; Hijacking with Misuse; Data theft; Disruption; Hijacking with Misuse

name: Medusa ransomware group compromised and stole data from El Cruce hospital in Argentina in January 2025
description:
Medusa ransomware group compromised and stole data from Hospital de Alta Complejidad El Cruce “Néstor Kirchner” in Florencio Varela, Buenos Aires Province in January 2025.
The attack, carried out by one of Medusa’s affiliates, compromised and lost over 760GB of sensitive data. As a result, the hospital’s IT department has taken its official website offline to contain the incident.
Medusa is demanding a ransom of $200,000 in Bitcoin for deleting the stolen files, with a payment deadline set for February 6. The file tree published on Medusa’s blog on the Tor network reveals more than 650,000 lin

name: Unknown Threat Actors Blocked Internet Connection at Torre del Greco Municipality in Italy on 30 January 2025
description: On 30 January 2025, hackers attacked the municipality of Torre del Greco, causing a computer blackout that lasted over 24 hours. The attack, which occurred from Thursday afternoon to the next day, resulted in a lack of internet connection and slowed down work in all sectors, including the urban police. The registry offices were forced to close and Mayor Luigi Mennella postponed a junta session. Specialist technicians investigated the cause of the attack, which was suspected to be either a failure, sabotage, or a hacking attempt.

name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched a Ransomware Attack on Tata Technologies in India on 31 January 2025
description: Tata Technologies Ltd., an Indian public multinational tech firm, was hit by a ransomware attack that impacted the company network on 31 January 2025. The attack, reported to India's national stock exchange, temporarily affected some of the company's IT assets. Client delivery services remained fully operational. As a precautionary measure, some IT services were suspended, but have now been restored. A detailed investigation is underway in collaboration with cybersecurity experts. name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched a DDoS Attack on German Railway Providers Metronom, Erixx, and Enno on 27 January 2025
description: On 27 January 2025, a cyber attack was launched against the websites of German railway providers Metronom, Erixx, and Enno, resulting in their websites being inaccessible. The attack, suspected to be a DDoS attack, involved a botnet sending numerous requests to the websites, causing them to collapse due to overload. The affected websites include www.dermetronom.de, www.erixx.de, and www.derenno.de. Metronom spokesperson Simon Mrtens informed the Abendblatt about the incident. Despite countermeasures being taken, the websites were expected to remain restricted until the weekend. The attack did

description: On 26 January 2025, New York Blood Center Enterprises (NYBCe) was hit by a ransomware attack, disrupting critical blood donation services across the US. The attack was identified and confirmed by NYBCe and third-party cybersecurity experts. Law enforcement was notified. No information was provided about the identity of the perpetrator or whether any data was accessed. The incident was disclosed by NYBCe in a statement on 29 January 2025. description: On 30 January 2025, Ukraine's cyber forces, specifically a group from Military Intelligence , launched a DDoS attack on the digital infrastructure of Russian companies Gazprom and Gazprom Neft, on the anniversary of the Heroes of Kruty battle. The attack, which started on 28 January 2025, targeted online services of enterprises that support the Russian army, resulting in Gazprom customers being unable to access their personal accounts, use the company's online services, pay for fuel with fuel cards, or accumulate and redeem bonus points on their accounts. name: Unknown threat actors disrupted strassen.gr.ch website in Switzerland on 28 January 2025
description: On 28 January 2025, the strassen.gr.ch website, run by the Civil Engineering Office of the Canton of Graubünden in Switzerland, was the victim of a cyber attack. The attack occurred between 10:00 and 15:30, making road map data inaccessible.

name: Unknown threat actors disrupted access to computer systems and the website of Ecuador's national civil registry DIGERCIC on 28 January 2025
description: The General Directorate of Civil Registry, Identification and Certification of Ecuador (DIGERCIC) was targeted in what appeared to be a DDoS attack originating from IP addresses in Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia, which affected access to online services, including the appointment scheduling system for issuing identigy cards and passports.  The institution filed a complaint with the Pichincha Prosecutors Office on 28 January 2025, to investigate the incident and establish the responsibilities of the perpetrators and accomplices.

name: Ukraine Military Intelligence HUR disrupted Russian telecommunicaton provider MegaFon beginning 25 January 2025
description: Ukraine Military Intelligence service HUR disrupted Russian telecommunication provider MegaFon on 25 January 2025. Sources from the HUR told Kyiv Post that this was done through a DDoS attack. Disruptions were also noted for other operators, namely Yota and NetByNet and also acknowledged by Russian communication agency, Roskomnadzor.

name: Unknown threat actors breached the South African Weather Service in South Africa on 26 January 2025
description: The South African Weather Service was targeted by cybercriminals over two days, resulting in the disruption of its email system and website. The attack occurred on Saturday and Sunday, with the ICT systems going down on Sunday evening.

name: Unknown threat actors gained access to engineering company Smiths Group in the United Kingdom
description: On 28 January 2025, Smiths Group, a British multinational engineering company, disclosed a security breach to the London Stock Exchange after attackers gained unauthorized access to the company's systems.  Smiths Group isolated affected systems and activated business continuity plans. The company is working with cybersecurity experts to recover affected systems and determine the impact on the business.

name: Iran-linked hacktivist group Handala breached and hijacked emergency systems in kindergartens across Israel, broadcasting rocket sirens and Arabic songs on 27 January 2025
description: On 27 January 2025, a pro-Palestinian hacker group called Handala breached the emergency systems used in Israeli schools, including approximately 20 kindergartens, and broadcast rocket sirens and Arabic songs that Israel's cyber agency called supportive of terrorism. The hackers claimed to have compromised MaagarTec, an Israeli electronics firm that operates panic button systems in schools, and wiped the company's system after sending the alerts. Local media reported the incident, and the Israel National Cyber Directorate confirmed its collaboration with the company and the Ministry of Educati

name: Unknown threat actors breached Matagorda County government in Texas starting on 24 January 2025
description: On 27 January 2025, Matagorda County government in Texas, serving about 40,000 residents, suffered a cyberattack that forced officials to declare a disaster. The attack, discovered on 24 January 2025, involved unauthorized access to internal systems, impacting various departments and disrupting some operations. With assistance from cybersecurity experts, the Department of Public Safety Cybersecurity Division, the Texas Department of Emergency Management, and the Department of Informational Services, the breach was contained to internal county systems. The FBI was notified of the incident. Mata

name: Unknown Threat Actors Launched a Cyber Attack on Tbilisi Public Transport Passenger Payment System in Georgia, Causing It to Malfunction and Play Unexpected Audio Recordings On 24 January 2025
description: On 24 January 2025, a cyber attack was carried out on the public transport passenger payment system in Tbilisi, causing it to malfunction and generate error messages, as well as play audio recordings, including the anthems of Georgia and the European Union and voice messages from politicians. The system was restored by representatives from the Bank of Georgia and Azri Ltd.

name: Lockbit Used Ransomware to Partially Encrypt and Exfiltrate Data from 45 German Schools in Rhineland-Palatinate as of 14 January 2025
description: On 14-15 January 2025, LockBit gained unauthorized access to the IT systems of Topack IT solutions GmbH. The provider was responsible for the IT infrastructure of at least 45 schools in Speyer, Germersheim, Bad Dürkheim, and Neustadt. LockBit 3.0 used ransomware to partially encrypt and exfiltrate data from several schools. The attackers demanded ransom in the form of cryptocurrencies to release the encrypted data, threatening to publish confidential information on the darknet if their demands were not met. By 30 January 2025, the captured data would be offered for sale. The Landeskriminalamt

description: On 24 January 2025, Singapore-based cryptocurrency exchange Phemex was forced to suspend some of its operations after a suspected compromise resulted in the theft of over $69 million in crypto assets. Phemex did not immediately disclose whether a vulnerability in its security infrastructure was exploited. On 22 February, blockchain analyst ZachXBT identified an overlap in wallet addresses that linked the Lazarus Group to the incident.

IntelBroker claimed to have had access to HPE's API, WePay, and GitHub repositories for at least two days. HPE immediately launched an investigation and activated their cyber response protocols. name: Unknown Threat Actors Hacked the Cuban Ministry of Foreign Affairs X Account in Cuba on 20 January 2025
description: The Cuban Foreign Ministry's account on social network X was hacked on 20 January 2025. The hackers took control of the account after 08:00 local time. Any information published from the CubaMINREX account after that time was false. The Cuban Foreign Ministry denounced the hack on 20 January 2025, and warned the public about the false information through a message posted on Facebook.

description: On Friday, 17 January 2025, Blacon High School in Melbourne Road, Chester, was targeted by a ransomware attack, which encrypted files and data, and demanded money in exchange for decrypting the affected system or files. The school was forced to close for two days, Monday, 20 January 2025, and Tuesday, 21 January 2025, to deal with the attack. Headteacher Rachel Hudson informed parents and carers about the incident through a statement on the school's website. name: Unknown threat actors launched a Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack on LuxTrust in Luxembourg on 15 January 2025
description:
LuxTrust was targeted by a Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack on Wednesday, January 15, 2025. The attack made its verification services unavailable for around half an hour, from 11:05, and was confirmed by a LuxTrust spokesperson in an email to the Luxembourg Times on the same day.
This attack followed a similar DDoS attack last Friday, which disrupted several government websites and connected services, including LuxTrust, for two hours.

North Korean state-sponsored hacking group Lazarus Group compromised Web3 and virtual currency developers in numerous countries beginning on 9 January 2025 at the latest. Unveiled the same day by the SecurityScorecard STRIKE team and further detailed in a 15 January report, the campaign involved fake recruiters on platforms such as LinkedIn who tricked developers into cloning malicious GitLab repositories. These repositories infected systems with modular malware capable of keylogging, clipboard monitoring, file exfiltration, and browser credential theft.

name: Unknown threat actors launched a DDoS attack on the Swiss federal government websites on 10 January 2025
description: On Friday, 10 January 2025, the Swiss federal government was targeted by a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) cyber attack, which caused numerous federal websites to be unavailable for approximately 45 minutes. The attack, which was confirmed by the Federal IT Bureau (BIT), affected various websites, phone services, and Outlook, as well as other specialized applications. The BIT and the Federal Office for Cyber Security (BACS) took countermeasures to stabilize the situation and are currently further investigating the incident. According to the BIT, no data was compromised during the attack.

name: Pro-Russian threat actor NoName057(16) carried out DDoS attacks on Italian public institutions and companies in Italy on 11 and 12 January 2025
description: On 11 January 2025, the pro-Russian cyber criminal group NoName057(16) carried out a series of cyber attacks on Italian public institutions and companies. The attack targeted the websites of the ministries of Foreign Affairs, Infrastructure, and Transport, as well as the Carabinieri, Navy, and Air Force. Local public transport companies, including Atac of Rome, Amat of Palermo, and Amt of Genoa, were also affected. The group used DDoS attacks, causing temporary slowdowns in services. name: Hacktivist group Ukrainian Cyber Alliance breached Russian ISP Nodex, stole sensitive documents and wiped systems on 7 January 2025
description: On January 9, 2025, the Ukrainian Cyber Alliance, a pro-Ukraine hacktivist group, breached Russian ISP Nodex, stealing sensitive documents and wiping systems, thereby destroying the company's connectivity. The attack was confirmed by Nodex, who speculated the involvement of Russia-linked threat actors, and by internet monitoring service NetBlocks, which reported a disruption in Nodex's connectivity. incident_type: Data theft; Disruption; Hijacking with Misuse
initiator_name: Cyber Resistance aka the Ukrainian Cyber Alliance

name: Unknown Threat Actors Disrupted The Upper Canada District School Board Network In Canada On 5 January 2025
description: On 5 January 2025, the Upper Canada District School Board was targeted by a cyber attack, disrupting its network and preventing all schools under its jurisdiction from accessing the internet. The board contacted police and worked with trusted experts to assess the impact and restore services. The board informed the public about the attack on 6 January 2025. incident_type: Data theft; Disruption; Hijacking with Misuse

name: Unknown Threat Actors Breached Kingston Police Service In Canada On 5 January 2025
description: On 5 January 2025, the Kingston Police Service in Ontario, Canada, was affected by a cyber incident that impacted some of its IT systems, particularly non-emergency functions. The incident was first identified on Friday, 5 January 2025, and reported by Kingston police on 6 January 2025. The Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) Cybercrime Investigations Team is investigating the incident, with the assistance of third-party cybersecurity experts. Kingston police immediately implemented mitigation strategies to protect the public, staff, data, and organization. The Information and Privacy Commissioner
source_incident_detection_disclosure: Incident disclosed by authorities of victim state

name: The threat group called Illegal Platform hacked Manisa News Agency and Manisa Pocket TV in Turkey on January 5, 2025
description: On January 5, 2025, Manisa News Agency and Manisa Pocket TV were attacked by the 'Illegal Platform' group. The attackers used a photo of Soma Mayor Sercan Okur and posted anticapitalism statements. Mayor Okur denied any involvement, stating his name and photo were used without his consent, and announced he would follow the legal process. Manisa News Agency officials filed a criminal complaint. The 'Illegal Platform' group claimed the incident, stating they were not affiliated with other organizations like Anonymous.

name: Unknown threat actors launched a DDoS attack on NTT Docomo in Japan on 2 January 2025
description: On 2 January 2025, Japan's largest mobile carrier, NTT Docomo, was targeted by a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack. The attack temporarily disrupted some of its services, including its news website, video streaming platform, mobile payment, webmail services, and a golf enthusiasts website. These services were unavailable from early Thursday morning until late afternoon. NTT Docomo reported the incident and worked to restore full operations, with access to most services restored, although some content updates might still face delays.
